title: narcissus tazetta subsp. gussonei
intro: Narcissus tazetta subsp. gussonei, also known as the Italian daffodil, is a type of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and strong, sweet fragrance. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to treat respiratory issues and act as an expectorant to help relieve congestion. The health benefits of Narcissus tazetta subsp. gussonei include its ability to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
